iti98:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti98:2.1: “Monks, there are these two kinds of gifts:
iti98:2.2: a gift of material things & a gift of the Dhamma.
iti98:2.3:
Of these two kinds of gifts, this is supreme:
a gift of the Dhamma.
iti98:3.1: There are these two kinds of sharing:
iti98:3.2: sharing of material things & sharing of the Dhamma.
iti98:3.3:
Of these two kinds of sharing, this is supreme:
sharing of the Dhamma.
iti98:4.1: There are these two kinds of assistance:
iti98:4.2: assistance with material things & assistance with the Dhamma.
iti98:4.3:
Of these two kinds of assistance, this is supreme:
assistance with the Dhamma.”
iti98:5.1: The gift he describes as supreme & unsurpassed,
iti98:5.2: the sharing the Blessed One has extolled:
iti98:5.3: who–confident in the supreme field of merit, wise, discerning–
iti98:5.4: wouldn’t give it at appropriate times?
iti98:6.1: Both for those who proclaim it and those who listen,
iti98:6.2: confident in the message of the One Well-Gone:
iti98:6.4: those heeding the message of the One Well-Gone.
